steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content: Start with $$y=7x^2-bx-6.$$ Factor $7$ from the $x$ terms: $$y=7\left(x^2-\frac{b}{7}x\right)-6.$$ Half of $\frac{b}{7}$ is $\frac{b}{14}$, so add and subtract $\left(\frac{b}{14}\right)^2$ inside the parentheses.

steps.title: Match the equivalent expression
steps.content: The derived form exactly matches Choice A: $$y=7\left(x-\frac{b}{14}\right)^2-6-\frac{b^2}{28}.$$ Therefore, A is correct.
